data.content:
## Implementation Plan

### Summary
Add an 'Acceptance criteria' column to the active items table. The acceptance field already exists on the Beat type (src/lib/types.ts:68), so no data model changes are needed.

### Complexity: Low
Single-feature, 2-3 files changed. Follows existing column patterns closely.

### Steps

**Step 1: Define the acceptance criteria column**
- File: src/components/beat-column-defs-extra.tsx
- Add acceptanceColumn() function following existing column patterns
- Column config: accessorKey: 'acceptance', header: 'Acceptance criteria'
- Sizing: size: 180, minSize: 120, maxSize: 280
- Cell: render row.original.acceptance with truncation (truncate class) and title tooltip
- Use text-xs text-muted-foreground styling consistent with other text columns

**Step 2: Insert column into active view column order**
- File: src/components/beat-columns.tsx
- Import acceptanceColumn from beat-column-defs-extra
- Insert after idColumn() (position index 2) — this is the 2nd data column after select checkbox
- Guard with if (showAgent) so it only appears in the active/queues view

**Step 3: Verify responsive behavior**
- Table container already uses overflow-x-auto for horizontal scroll on narrow viewports
- Column min/max sizing ensures appropriate adaptation
- No additional responsive work expected

### Dependencies & Risks
- None. The acceptance field is already on the Beat type and populated by the API.
- If most beats have empty acceptance criteria, column may appear mostly blank — acceptable.

### Test Strategy
- Run existing Playwright tests (bun run test) for regression check
- Verify column position, header text, and content rendering
- Verify truncation and tooltip for long text

### Files Changed
1. src/components/beat-column-defs-extra.tsx — new acceptanceColumn() function
2. src/components/beat-columns.tsx — import and insert column
